Post Footer: User Info & Reactions #### **Post Header/Footer Container** (`.card-icons-container`) Location: `postcards.css` lines 597-641 ``` .card-icons-container { display: flex flex-wrap: wrap width: 100% padding: 0.5rem 1.15rem 0.85rem 1.15rem min-height: 22px border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card) gap: 0.5rem } ``` #### **A. User Info Section** (`.user-info-child-container`) - **Avatar** (`.user-info-avatar`): 22px × 22px, circular - Badge rings for "legend" (gold #FFD700) and "og" (purple #9B59FF) users - Glow animations with keyframes - **Username** (`.user-newsfeed-name`): 0.78rem, 600 weight - Hover: underline + sage accent color - **Verified Badge**: `` - **Separator**: "•" dot - **Posted Date** (`.user-post-date`): Formatted timestamp #### **B. Reaction Buttons** (Icon container) Four action buttons configured in `cardIcons` array: 1. **Like Button** (`.like-button`) - SVG: Heart icon (2-path fill strategy) - Color when liked: `rgb(255, 116, 116)` (red) - Hover bg: `rgba(255, 161, 161, 0.12)` - Count: Formatted via `formatCounts()` 2. **Comment Button** (`.comment-button`) - SVG: Chat bubble - Color hover: `var(--accent-amber)` (#D4A853) - Action: Opens content detail view via `viewContent()` 3. **Bookmark Button** (`.bookmark-button`) - SVG: Bookmark icon - Color when bookmarked: `rgb(72, 208, 135)` (sage green) - Action: Debounced `handleClickBookmark()` with 2.5s toast message 4. **View Count** (`.icon-container #card-icons-view`) - SVG: Eye icon - Non-interactive display of `journal?.views` #### **C. Reading Time Pill** (`.reading-time-container`) - Margin-left: auto (pushed right) - Styling: Pill shape (10px border-radius) - Format: "X min(s) read" - Font: 0.62rem, 550 weight, sage accent color #### **D. Settings Menu** (`.user-post-settings`) - Three-dot menu icon (SVG) - Opens `.setting-modal` on click (currently minimal implementation) --- ### 4. Canvas Preview Component Details **File:** `C:/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/CanvasPreview/CanvasPreview.jsx` **Props:** `canvasDoc` (parsed canvas JSON from database) **Rendering Logic:** 1. Parses `canvasDoc` via `parseCanvasDoc()` utility 2. Lazy loads via IntersectionObserver (rootMargin: 320px) 3. Responsive sizing: ResizeObserver watches container 4. Renders `` component with: - Viewport dimensions (responsive to container) - Stage dimensions: 560px base width, height calculated by aspect ratio - Fit scale calculation for centering - Grid dots overlay (opacity 0.35) - Doodles, images, text snippets from canvas data **CSS Styling** (`canvaspreview.css`): - Max height: 220px (desktop), 160px (mobile) - Aspect ratios: 1:1 or 4:5 - Grid dots pattern: 16×16px spacing - Loading state: "Loading canvas..." text - Empty state: "Canvas" text (centered) --- ### 6. Remix Button Functionality **Handler:** `handleRemixCanvas()` in PostCards.jsx (lines 106-130) ```jsx const handleRemixCanvas = (e, journal) => { e.stopPropagation(); // Auth check if(!session) { openAuthModal(); return; } // Generate remix title (prepend "Remix: " if not already) const sourceTitle = journal?.title?.trim() || ''; const remixTitle = sourceTitle.toLowerCase().startsWith('remix:') ? sourceTitle : `Remix: ${sourceTitle || 'Canvas'}`; // Navigate to editor with canvas pre-loaded navigate('/home', { state: { openEditor: true, editorMode: 'canvas', initialTitle: remixTitle, initialCanvasDoc: journal?.canvas_doc || null, remixSource: { journalId: journal?.id, authorName: journal?.users?.name || 'Unknown' } } }); }; ``` **Styling:** (postcards.css lines 574-594) - Button: `.canvas-card-action-btn.is-remix` - Border color: Purple-tinted (42% opacity of accent-purple) - Text color: `var(--accent-purple)` - Hover: Sage accent override --- ### 7. Profile Post Cards Layout (Grid & List Views) **Grid View** (profilepostcards.css lines 85-170): - 3-column layout on desktop, 2-column on mobile - Image aspect ratio: 4:3 - Minimal text display (2-line title, 2-line snippet) - Footer: privacy icon + settings menu **List View** (profilepostcards.css lines 212-323): - Full-width cards (similar to feed) - Canvas preview supported - Author info + engagement data - Matches main feed card styling --- ### 8. CSS Files Summary | File | Purpose | Key Classes | |------|---------|-------------| | `postcards.css` | Main feed card styling | `.cards`, `.card-content`, `.card-icons-container`, `.canvas-type-badge`, `.canvas-card-actions` | | `canvaspreview.css` | Canvas preview theming | `.canvas-preview`, `.canvas-preview-dots`, `.canvas-preview-stage`, `.is-canvas-card` | | `profilepostcards.css` | Profile view cards | `.profile-postcards`, `.postcards-grid-view`, `.postcards-list-view`, `.post-settings-container` | --- ### 9. Data Flow 1. **PostCards Component:** - Fetches journals via `useInfiniteQuery` (React Query) - Detects post type: `journal?.post_type === 'canvas'` - Extracts content via `ParseContent()` or `getCanvasPreview()` - Renders appropriate preview (image banner or canvas) 2. **User Interactions:** - Like: `useLikeMutation()` with real-time update - Bookmark: `useBookMarkMutation()` with 2.5s toast - Comment: `viewContent()` navigates to detail view - Remix: `handleRemixCanvas()` opens editor with pre-filled data - Views: `useAddViewsMutation()` increments view count 3. **State Management:** - Auth: Context via `useAuth()` - Queries: React Query caching + infinite scroll pagination - UI: Local state for modals, search, view toggle --- ### 10. Key Component Relationships ``` PostCards (main feed) ├── CanvasPreview (renders canvas artwork) └── card-icons-container (footer) ├── user-info-child-container (avatar + name) └── action buttons (like, comment, bookmark, view count) ProfilePostCards ├── Grid View │ └── postcards-grid-item (3-column) │ ├── CanvasPreview or Image │ └── postcards-grid-body └── List View └── profile-postcards (full-width) ├── CanvasPreview or Image └── card-icons-container (same as feed) ``` --- ## Summary The post card system is a well-structured, dual-view design supporting both traditional text/image posts and interactive Canvas artwork. The Remix functionality allows users to copy and modify canvas creations, with the button styled distinctly in purple/sage accent colors and positioned in the canvas-specific action bar. The layout is responsive with mobile-specific optimizations and uses Framer Motion for smooth animations throughout.

- `privacy` - `'public'` or `'private'`

**Optional Remix Fields:**
- `is_remix` - Boolean flag indicating if post is a remix
- `remix_source_journal_id` - UUID reference to the source canvas post

**Canvas Document Structure** (`canvas_doc` JSON):
```javascript
{
  version: 1,
  meta: {
    aspectRatio: '1:1' | '4:5',
    gridEnabled: boolean,
    theme: 'light' | 'dark'
  },
  snippets: [
    {
      id: string,
      text: string,
      x: number (0-1),
      y: number (0-1),
      rotation: number,
      zIndex: number,
      fontStyle: 'serif' | 'handwritten' | 'bold' | 'serifDisplay',
      scaleX: 1 | -1,
      sizeScale: number (0.6-2.5)
    }
  ],
  images: [
    {
      id: string,
      src: string (URL),
      x: number (0-1),
      y: number (0-1),
      width: number (0.08-1),
      height: number (0.08-1.2),
      rotation: number,
      zIndex: number,
      scaleX: 1 | -1
    }
  ],
  doodles: [
    {
      id: string,
      points: number[] (normalized 0-1 coordinates),
      color: string (hex color),
      size: number (1-14)
    }
  ]
}
```

### 2. API Endpoints for Canvas Posts

**Location:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\server\routes\routes.js`

  - Required auth

  - Controller: `createCanvasRemixController`

- `GET /journals/canvas/gallery` - Fetch canvas posts gallery (line 143)
  - Optional params: `limit` (1-72, default 36), `sort` ('hottest' or 'newest'), `cursor`, `userId`
  - Controller: `getCanvasGalleryController`

- `GET /journal/:journalId` - Fetch single post by ID (line 145)
  - Supports both text and canvas posts
  - Query param: `userId` (optional, for interaction flags)
  - Controller: `getJournalByIdController`

- `POST /save-journal` - Create a new post (line 137)
  - Required auth
  - Supports both text and canvas posts via `post_type` param
  - Body: `{ title, content, postType, canvasDoc, remixSourceJournalId, isRemix }`

### 3. Feed/Home Page Rendering

**Location:** `C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\PostCards.jsx`

**Key rendering logic:**

- Posts are fetched via `getJournals()` API call
- `PostCards` component maps over journal list and renders each post
- Canvas posts are detected via `journal?.post_type === 'canvas'`

**Canvas-specific rendering:**
- `CanvasPreview` component (lines 21, 103-104) - renders a small preview of the canvas
- `handleExpandCanvas()` function (lines 83-104) - opens full canvas in content viewer
- `handleRemixCanvas()` function (lines 106-130) - creates a remix by navigating to editor with initial canvas doc

**Key interaction functions:**
```javascript

handleExpandCanvas = (e, journal) => {

    journal.users.id,
    journal.users.name,

}

// Line 106-130

    state: {
      openEditor: true,
      editorMode: 'canvas',
      initialTitle: remixTitle,
      initialCanvasDoc: journal?.canvas_doc || null,
      remixSource: {
        journalId: journal?.id,
        authorName: journal?.users?.name || 'Unknown'
      }
    }
  });
}
```

### 4. Distinction Between Text & Canvas Posts

**In rendering logic** (`ContentView.jsx`, lines 101-159):

- Post type is determined from `journal?.post_type` or inferred from presence of `canvasDoc`

   ```javascript
   export const createCanvasRemixService = async({sourceJournalId, userId, titleOverride = ''}) => {
     // Validates source journal is canvas type and accessible
     // Extracts plain text from canvas snippets
     // Generates embeddings for search
     // Creates new journal with is_remix=true and remix_source_journal_id
   }
   ```

3. **Database fields populated:**
   - `is_remix: true`
   - `remix_source_journal_id: <sourceJournalId>`
   - `title: 'Remix: <original_title>'` (unless `titleOverride` provided)
   - `canvas_doc: <copied from source>`
   - `user_id: <remixing user's id>`

**Remix validation:**
- Source journal must exist
- Source must be a canvas post (`post_type === 'canvas'`)
- Source must be public or owned by viewer
- Permissions: Private canvas posts only accessible to owner

### 6. Post Metadata Storage & Display

**Metadata fields:**
- `title` - Displayed in post cards and content viewer
- Text preview: 
  - Canvas: `getCanvasPreview()` extracts first 215 chars of concatenated snippet text
  - Text: Parsed from Lexical content

**Display location:** `PostCards.jsx`
- Title shown in card header
- Preview text shown below title
- Reading time calculated via `CalculateText()` helper

User Views Feed
    ↓
getJournals() API → Returns journals with post_type & canvas_doc
    ↓
PostCards Component renders each journal
    ↓

    ↓

  - Shows comments, likes, bookmarks

  - Shows comments, likes, bookmarks
```

Footer: User info (avatar + name + date) + like/comment/bookmark/view icons + reading time + settings dots ### Desired Layout (from screenshot, top to bottom): 1. **Header**: User avatar + username + timestamp + verified badge + three-dot settings menu (right-aligned) 2. **Title**: Large bold text 3. **Body text**: Excerpt/preview text 4. **Canvas preview**: Artwork 5. **Canvas label**: "CANVAS · {description}" below the artwork 6. **Footer**: Current like/comment/bookmark/view icons + "Remix this Canvas" button (right-aligned) ### User Decisions: - Keep current reaction icons (like/comment/bookmark/view), just reorganize layout - Add a NEW optional "canvas description" field that authors can set - Layout changes apply ONLY to canvas posts; text posts stay the same ### Key Files: - **Post card component**: `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/PostCards.jsx` (lines 521-674) - renders each card - **Canvas preview component**: `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/CanvasPreview/CanvasPreview.jsx` - **Canvas preview CSS**: `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/CanvasPreview/canvaspreview.css` - **Post cards CSS**: `client/src/components/HomePage/postCards/postcards.css` - **Canvas editor**: `client/src/components/HomePage/Canvas/CanvasEditor.jsx` - where users create canvases - **API client**: `client/API/Api.js` - API calls - **Save journal endpoint**: `server/routes/routes.js` → controllers → services - **Upload service**: `server/services/uploadService.js` - handles journal creation ### Current Card JSX Structure (PostCards.jsx lines 531-672): ```jsx {/* 1. Canvas Preview at top */} {/* 2. Card content */}
Canvas

{title}

{previewText}

{/* Remix button */}
{/* 3. Footer */}
{/* Avatar, username, verified badge, date */} {/* Like, comment, bookmark, view icons */}
...
...
 ``` ### Canvas Doc Structure: ```javascript { version: 1, meta: { aspectRatio: '1:1' | '4:5', gridEnabled: boolean, theme: 'light' | 'dark' }, snippets: [...], images: [...], doodles: [...] } ``` ## Design Requirements Please design a detailed implementation plan that covers: 1. **JSX Restructure** (PostCards.jsx): How to reorganize the canvas card sections to match the desired layout. Be specific about which elements move where. The non-canvas cards should remain untouched. 2. **New Description Field**: - Where to store it (probably `canvas_doc.meta.description` in the JSON, or a separate column) - How to add it to the canvas editor UI - How to pass it through the save flow - How to display it in the card ("CANVAS · {description}") 3. **CSS Changes**: What styling needs to change for the new layout - the user info at top, canvas preview repositioned, label styling, footer with remix button alongside icons. 4. **Profile/Visit Profile Cards**: Whether similar changes are needed for `ProfilePostCards.jsx` and `VisitedProfilePostCards.jsx` which also render canvas cards. Consider: - Keep changes minimal - only restructure the canvas card, don't touch text post rendering - The canvas description should be optional (can be empty/null) - The "CANVAS" label should always show even if no description is provided - Mobile responsiveness
